author: lOrm
content:
## OC current-behavior spec (HEAD ba62e46d) — Phase-2 pin

Describes the memdir entrypoint and background-job scheduler subsystems as implemented TODAY.

## B1 — `load_entrypoint(cwd)` discovery search order
**OC location**: `src/memdir/entrypoint.rs:91-117` (`load_entrypoint`), `:122-130` (`discovery_candidates`).
**Contract**: `(cwd: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<Option<EntrypointFile>>`. Tries in order: (1) `<cwd>/MEMORY.md`, (2) `<cwd>/.openclaudia/MEMORY.md`, (3) `<HOME>/.openclaudia/MEMORY.md` (omitted silently if `dirs::home_dir()` returns `None`). For each path: `std::fs::read_to_string`. On `Ok(raw)` → run `truncate_content(&raw)`, return `Ok(Some(EntrypointFile { path, content, truncation }))`. On `Err(NotFound)` → continue to next candidate. On ANY OTHER `Err` (EACCES, EIO, invalid UTF-8) → propagate as `Err` via `with_context("MEMORY.md candidate {} exists but is unreadable")` (fix #740). After all candidates exhausted → `Ok(None)`.
**Edge cases**: Symlinks are followed by `read_to_string` (no special handling). Empty file returns `Some(EntrypointFile { content: "", truncation: None })` — distinct from `None`. `HOME` unset silently drops candidate (3) but does NOT error.
**Error paths**: First non-`NotFound` IO error propagates with anyhow context — silent fallthrough on a permission denial would have injected the wrong MEMORY.md into the system prompt (the #740 bypass that this guards against).
**Invariants**: First-hit-wins is strict: `<cwd>/MEMORY.md` ALWAYS shadows `<cwd>/.openclaudia/MEMORY.md` when both exist.

## B2 — Line truncation: `MAX_ENTRYPOINT_LINES = 200`
**OC location**: `src/memdir/entrypoint.rs:138-167` (`truncate_content`), `:26` (constant).
**Contract**: Inside `truncate_content(raw: &str) -> (String, EntrypointTruncation)`. `line_count = raw.lines().count()` (no pre-trim). `byte_count = raw.len()`. If `line_count <= 200 && byte_count <= 25_000` → `(raw.to_string(), None)` early-return. Otherwise: when `line_count > 200`, walk `raw.split_inclusive('\n')` keeping the first 200 segments; the resulting string preserves CRLF terminators and trailing newlines verbatim (fix #744 — `lines()` + `join("\n")` would have lossily collapsed line endings).
**Edge cases**: Exactly 200 lines → no truncation (boundary is `> 200`). File with trailing `\n`: `raw.lines()` does NOT yield a trailing empty entry, so a 200-line file ending in `\n` is `line_count == 200` and not truncated.
**Invariants**: `split_inclusive('\n')` preserves byte-exact original terminators; no rewriting.

## B3 — Byte truncation: `MAX_ENTRYPOINT_BYTES = 25_000`, UTF-8-safe
**OC location**: `src/memdir/entrypoint.rs:168-171` (apply step), `:189-202` (`utf8_safe_truncate`).
**Contract**: Byte truncation runs AFTER line truncation, on the line-truncated string. If `truncated.len() > 25_000`, replace with `utf8_safe_truncate(&truncated, 25_000)`. `utf8_safe_truncate(s, max_bytes)`: if `s.len() <= max_bytes` returns `s.to_string()`; otherwise sets `end = max_bytes` and decrements while `!s.is_char_boundary(end) && end > 0`. Cuts at the NEAREST char boundary — may cut mid-line but never mid-codepoint.
**Edge cases**: Multi-byte boundaries (2/3/4-byte sequences) all handled by `is_char_boundary` decrement loop. A pure-ASCII 25_000-byte file is kept as-is. A 4-byte emoji straddling byte 25_000 is rolled back to a valid boundary at or before 25_000.

## B4 — Truncation suffix and `EntrypointTruncation` variant
**OC location**: `src/memdir/entrypoint.rs:173-186`.
**Contract**: When ANY truncation fires, the literal string `"\n\n[truncated — MEMORY.md exceeded the display limits]"` is appended to `truncated`. The variant is decided by `(lines_triggered, bytes_triggered)`: `(true, true)` → `LinesAndBytes`, `(true, false)` → `Lines`, `(false, true)` → `Bytes`, `(false, false)` → `None` (defensive — unreachable inside this branch). `EntrypointFile::was_truncated()` returns `true` iff variant is NOT `None`.
**Edge cases**: Defensive `(false, false)` match arm is unreachable inside the post-early-return block; preserved for readability.

## B5 — `JobScheduler::tick` schedule-and-run
**OC location**: `src/services/background.rs:195-269`.
**Contract**: `JobScheduler { db: Arc<MemoryDb>, jobs: Vec<ScheduledJob> }`. `register(job, interval)` appends without setting `last_run`. `tick(&mut self) -> Vec<JobOutcome>`: snapshot `now = Instant::now()`. For each job in registration order: `due = last_run.is_none() || now.duration_since(last) >= interval`. If not due → skip. Otherwise SET `last_run = Some(now)` BEFORE calling `entry.job.run(&self.db)` (so a persistently failing job does not spin-loop). On `Ok(outcome)` log `info!` and push to result; on `Err(err)` log `warn!` but `last_run` is still updated. Returns successful outcomes only.
**Edge cases**: Errored jobs are retried only after `interval`. Tick is synchronous.
**Error paths**: Per-job errors are logged at `warn` and dropped; scheduler does not surface them.
**Invariants**: `BackgroundJob: Send + Sync` (compile-time via `Arc<dyn BackgroundJob>`). Trait is intentionally tokio-free.

## B6 — `MemoryConsolidationJob::run` two-pass consolidation
**OC location**: `src/services/background.rs:96-164`.
**Contract**: `name() == "memory_consolidation"`. Pass 1: `db.cleanup_expired_short_term()? -> (sessions_pruned, activities_pruned)`; `records_pruned = sum`. Pass 2: `dedup_archival(db)?` loads all archival entries via `db.memory_list(usize::MAX)`, builds `HashMap<content, (canonical_id, canonical_updated_at, all_ids)>` in one pass. Canonical = latest `updated_at`. Iterates each group's `all_ids` and `db.memory_delete(id)` for non-canonical; increments `deleted` only on `Ok(true)`. Returns `JobOutcome { job_name, records_pruned, records_deduped }`.
**Edge cases**: Identical content with identical timestamps — first-seen wins as canonical. O(N) memory in archival count.
**Error paths**: Any `db.*` error short-circuits with `?` — partial dedup possible if aborted mid-way.

## B7 — `EntrypointFile` shape
**OC location**: `src/memdir/entrypoint.rs:37-70`.
**Contract**: `EntrypointFile { path: PathBuf, content: String, truncation: EntrypointTruncation }`. `truncation` is `Copy + PartialEq + Eq` with variants `None | Lines | Bytes | LinesAndBytes`. `was_truncated()` is `!matches!(self.truncation, None)`. `path` records the candidate that hit.
**Invariants**: `content` is ALWAYS the truncated string — callers cannot accidentally re-truncate.
